# netbox_custom_object_type (Resource)

Manages a NetBox Custom Object Type, provided by the [netbox-custom-objects](https://github.com/netbox-community/netbox-custom-objects) plugin. A Custom Object Type defines a new object model in NetBox (its name, slug, and metadata); individual fields on the type are managed with a separate resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "netbox_custom_object_type" "example" {
  description         = "Logical role assigned to a server."
  group_name          = "Inventory"
  name                = "server_role"
  slug                = "server-role"
  tags                = ["managed-by-terraform"]
  verbose_name        = "Server Role"
  verbose_name_plural = "Server Roles"
  version             = "1.0.0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Internal name of the type. Must match `^[a-z0-9]+(_[a-z0-9]+)*$` (lowercase alphanumerics separated by underscores). Maximum 100 characters.
- `slug` (String) URL-friendly slug for the type. Must match `^[-a-zA-Z0-9_]+$`. Maximum 100 characters.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description of the type. Maximum 200 characters.
- `group_name` (String) Group label used to cluster similar Custom Object Types in the NetBox navigation menu.
- `tags` (Set of String) Set of NetBox tag slugs to associate with the type. Each entry must be an existing tag slug.
- `verbose_name` (String) Human-readable singular name shown in the NetBox UI.
- `verbose_name_plural` (String) Human-readable plural name shown in the NetBox UI.
- `version` (String) Free-form version string associated with the type's schema.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) RFC3339 timestamp when the type was created.
- `id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the Custom Object Type in NetBox.
- `last_updated` (String) RFC3339 timestamp of the last modification to the type.
- `object_type_name` (String) Dotted `app_label.model` name that NetBox uses to reference instances of this type internally.
- `table_model_name` (String) Dynamically generated Django model class name backing the type.
- `url` (String) API URL of this Custom Object Type as reported by NetBox.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
#!/bin/sh
# Custom Object Types are imported by their numeric NetBox ID.
terraform import netbox_custom_object_type.example 42
```
